title: Stacked Changes, Maintainer Workflows, and Integration Queues · Command Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs stacked changes, maintainer workflows, and integration queues from terse repository evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the stacked-change and maintainer integration protocol. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for stacked-change and maintainer integration protocol after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent dependent changes as explicit base and tip relationships, keep each review independently testable, and update downstream stacks after revision.
Use range-diff and interdiff-style evidence to explain rerolls, reordered commits, changed commit messages, and semantic deltas between series.
Compare topic-branch, integration-branch, subsystem, patch-flow, merge-queue, and release-train workflows by authority and recovery cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Gita Stacked Change Dependencies · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — Case G54 is a review in a disposable repository. the reroll changes only the requested validation patch and preserves stack dependencies. The learner must predict old and new series map by patch identity with reordered and amended entries visible, run git range-diff main...stack-v1 main...stack-v2, explain one patch changed, two map exactly, and the dependent tip moved to a new parent, test this changed condition: reorder the dependency after its consumer and reject the stack despite an equal final tree, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain both series refs and review comments until the integrated exact revision is published.

Which action demonstrates Gita Stacked Change Dependencies without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for represent dependent changes as explicit base and tip relationships, keep each review independently testable, and update downstream stacks after revision.
